choice: 12-month overlapping (circular) blocks, with block length configurable later alongside the draw-distribution config. Twelve months preserves within-year return autocorrelation and seasonality while still producing many distinct multi-year paths; circular/overlapping sampling avoids starving the tail of the series.
rationale: Monthly steps were chosen (90061ce7), so the bootstrap must draw monthly blocks. Block length is the key knob trading off autocorrelation capture vs. path diversity; 12 months is the common default. This rides on the existing 'make the draw distribution configurable' decision rather than adding a new config surface.
